steps.title: Use Vieta's formulas
steps.content:
For a quadratic $$x^2 + bx + c = 0$$, Vieta's formulas give the sum of the roots $$=-\frac{b}{a}$$ and the product $$=\frac{c}{a}$$ (where the leading coefficient is 1 here).
In $$x^2 - a x + 18 = 0$$ we have $$b = -a$$ and $$c = 18$$.
Therefore
$$\text{sum of roots}= -\frac{-a}{1}=a$$
$$\text{product of roots}= \frac{18}{1}=18$$

steps.title: Find integer factor pairs of 18
steps.content:
Since the roots are integers and their product is 18, we look for integer factor pairs of 18.
Both roots must be either both positive or both negative to give a positive product.
Because the sum equals $$a\gt0$$, the roots cannot both be negative (that would give a negative sum).
Thus we only consider positive pairs:
  • $$1\times18=18$$ -> sum $$1+18=19$$
  • $$2\times9=18$$ -> sum $$2+9=11$$
  • $$3\times6=18$$ -> sum $$3+6=9$$
Hence the possible values of $$a$$ (the sum) are 19, 11, and 9.

steps.title: Select a possible value
steps.content:
Choosing one of these, take $$a=11$$ (it satisfies all conditions).
The answer is 11.

Equivalent forms accepted: 9, 11, or 19.
